title: I Set Up a Claude Code Operating System for 6 Founders in 8 Hours
summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates setting up Claude Code as an AI Operating System for six non-technical founders in a single day. The setup includes a Context OS with four context files teaching Claude about business operations, enabling non-coders to build automation for invoicing, finance reporting, competitor analysis, and content workflows.
writeup: Liam Whatley recaps a one-day in-person mastermind in Cape Town where he walked six non-technical founders through setting up Claude Code as an AI operating system (AIOS) for their respective businesses, ranging from a holiday brokerage to a venture studio. The core thesis is that Claude Code is not a coding tool but a general-purpose super-agent that wraps any business model, freeing founders from operational work so they can adapt as AI compresses margins across every industry. The transcript covers the workshop curriculum (theory, environment setup) and the deliberate selection of participants spanning the widest possible range of technical backgrounds and business types to stress-test the methodology.
key_points: Claude Code functions as a universal AI operating system layer that sits around any business model rather than replacing it, capable of substituting for most existing AI tools and portions of the broader software stack.

The 'operator trap' describes founders spending 80% or more of their time working in the business rather than on it; the AIOS methodology is framed as the mechanism to break that pattern and reclaim strategic bandwidth.

Downward price pressure on services and products driven by AI is expected to compress margins significantly within 12 to 36 months, making adoption of this kind of system not optional but a survival requirement for maintaining competitiveness.

The six workshop participants were deliberately chosen for maximum diversity: technical range from ChatGPT-only user to AI agency owner, and business types including influencer marketing (30M GBP exit), web design, e-commerce and SaaS venture studio, voice AI agency, e-learning platform, and holiday brokerage.

Environment setup required installing Cursor, configuring Claude Code, and applying a starter kit template that provides the base structure for the operating system; Windows users introduced real-time process refinements not previously encountered in online delivery.
